title: aita for staying up late by myself?
text: my partner is *very* attention-demanding. i can't really engage in any private hobbies, such as reading or going online with my phone because she will talk constantly (which disrupts my concentration) or complain that i'm ignoring her. i haven't played a single video game since we've been together because i'm legit afraid she will start an argument. recently i've gotten back into reading books for pleasure. convenient for both of us because when she starts talking too much i can just pause where i'm at and i don't need too much focus, so she doesn't feel ignored. still mildly irritating for me though because a page i could finish in seconds will take me considerably longer to finish with her interruptions. example: last night we were lying in bed next to each other, both reading, and i had to constantly stop because she was giving me running commentary on the book *she* was reading. she goes to bed relatively early and i've been sleeping later and later, sometimes up to the am. it's not like i started doing it deliberately; sometimes when she's running on fumes i've just caught my second wind and am full of energy at night. coincidentally (or not), i've found myself enjoying the quiet "me time" staying up late brings. i can read or browse in peace and actually feel relieved now when she says she's going to bed, so i can have uninterrupted fun. the last few days, the missus has been complaining i don't go to bed with her. she said i'm being selfish and deliberately staying up late because i am "still acting like a bachelor". today, she actually said she feels like we've grown apart because we don't spend any "quality time" together, on the part of me staying up later than her. i'm flabbergastered. i don't mind giving her some quick kisses and cuddles before she goes to bed, but i don't feel comfortable holding her, in silence, *every night* until she falls asleep when i am not in the least bit tired. nor do i want to force myself to go to sleep with the feeling i haven't gotten the most out of my day. aita?

storyboard.content: My partner is very attention-demanding. I can't engage in private hobbies like reading or going on my phone because she will talk constantly or complain that I'm ignoring her.
I haven't played a single video game since we've been together because I'm afraid she will start an argument if I do.
Even when we're both reading in bed, she constantly interrupts me with running commentary on her own book. A page I could finish in seconds takes much longer with her interruptions.
I've been staying up later and later, sometimes into the early morning hours, after my partner goes to bed.
It's not like I started doing it deliberately. Sometimes when she's running on fumes I've caught my second wind and am full of energy at night.
I've found myself enjoying the quiet 'me time' staying up late brings. I can read or browse in peace and actually feel relieved when she says she's going to bed.
My partner has been complaining that I don't go to bed with her. She said I'm being selfish and 'still acting like a bachelor.'
She said she feels like we've grown apart because we don't spend any 'quality time' together, because of me staying up later than her.
I don't mind giving her quick kisses and cuddles before she goes to bed, but I don't feel comfortable holding her in silence every night until she falls asleep when I'm not the least bit tired.
